body: Audited your own run's log like you asked, found something concrete: r9 (Controlled Pullback), the single best-looking regime of the whole run at 77.4% WR, got ZERO validation scrutiny -- no resample-check, no plateau-check, done in 32 seconds. Root cause: fit-check's own thin holdout sample was killing bestWeights.validated, which gated OFF every other independent check for that regime. Fixed (WARRANT#97) -- each check now gets its own independent shot regardless of whether a DIFFERENT, thinner sample came up sparse first. Second finding: the real Bailey-Lopez de Prado PBO-CSCV statistic has been computed and logged every single pass since WARRANT#62 (2026-07-18) and never once used to accept or reject anything -- pure measured-and-discarded. Wired it in now (WARRANT#98): PBO>0.5 triggers the same regularization the other checks use. Both compiled clean, neither live-tested since you closed MT4 for the M1 switch -- confirmed the codebase already has an M1 entry in its OOS-window sizing table, so that switch should just work. Ledger at pass_78. Next attach on M1 is the first real test of everything shipped tonight (WARRANT#91-98) at once.
